description: Anti-pattern guard: nested Loop Over Items breaks at runtime — outer per-customer iteration plus inner paginated order fetch should use sub-workflow or HTTP pagination, not nested splitInBatches.

conversation.text: When the webhook fires with a customers array, for each customer fetch their orders from GET https://api.example.com/customers/{id}/orders (paginated, up to 5 pages each), then for each order fetch its line items from GET https://api.example.com/orders/{orderId}/lines. Process customers one at a time.

outcomeExpectations: Customers are processed one at a time (sequential outer iteration via default item flow or a single outer loop).
Order pagination uses HTTP Request built-in pagination (or a sub-workflow), not a nested Loop Over Items inside another Loop Over Items in the same workflow.
Line items are fetched per order without nesting two Split In Batches nodes in one workflow.

executionScenarios.name: one-customer-two-orders
executionScenarios.description: Single customer with two paginated orders and line items
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The webhook receives { "customers": [{ "id": "cust-1", "name": "Acme" }] }. This is a PAGINATED orders endpoint per customer — serve strictly by `page` query parameter; never return identical JSON for page=1 and page=2 or n8n stops with 'identical response 5x'. `GET https://api.example.com/customers/cust-1/orders?page=1` returns { "orders": [{ "id": "ord-1" }, { "id": "ord-2" }] }. `GET https://api.example.com/customers/cust-1/orders?page=2` returns { "orders": [] }. `GET https://api.example.com/orders/ord-1/lines` returns { "lines": [{ "sku": "A" }] }. `GET https://api.example.com/orders/ord-2/lines` returns { "lines": [{ "sku": "B" }] }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. Both orders (ord-1 and ord-2) are fetched for cust-1 and line items for both orders are retrieved (SKUs A and B appear in the run output or downstream processing). The workflow completes without loop wiring errors.
